data.title: Wall Street ended lower as investors assessed PCE inflation and GDP data
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes ended lower on Friday as investors digested a fresh batch of economic data, highlighted by the latest PCE inflation and GDP reports.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended -0.6%, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) closed -0.2%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) finished -0.9%. 

From a sector-by-sector stance, five of the 11 S&P sectors ended in the green, with utilities being the strongest-performing area of the session, while info tech was the weakest. 

“This week the Nasdaq tested its 200 day simple moving average from above, found support there, and closed flat on the week.  If you delete the words ‘Hormuz’ and ‘Strait Of’ from your consciousness, you would find that typically such a thing is bullish,” Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital stated.

On the economic front, core PCE inflation rose 3.1% Y/Y in January, a touch hotter than expected. Furthermore, the U.S. GDP growth estimate was revised lower to +0.7% in Q4. 

Additionally, durable goods orders growth has stalled in the month of January. The January job openings increased more than expected in the latest JOLTS report, and consumer sentiment data declined more than expected in March.

U.S. Treasury yields were muted. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was at 3.73%, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was at 4.28%, and the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was at 4.91%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Darden Restaurants (DRI) climbed +2%, and shares of Adobe (ADBE) fell -7.5%.

data.title: Court quashes Justice Department subpoenas against Fed Chair Powell - report
data.description:
Judge James Boasberg has blocked the subpoenas that the Justice Department served to the Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over his testimony before the Senate Banking Committee last June, CNBC News reported on March 13.

The testimony concerned a multi-year project to renovate historic Fed office buildings—a matter Powell characterized as merely a "pretext" for the administration's broader campaign against central bank independence.

Federal prosecutors had launched a criminal probe into Powell over the monetary authority's renovation of its Washington headquarters and whether Powell lied to Congress about the project’s scope during his congressional testimony last June.

"A mountain of evidence suggests that the Government served these subpoenas on the Board to pressure its Chair into voting for lower interest rates or resigning," Boasberg reportedly wrote in a court filing.

"On the other side of the scale, the Government has produced essentially zero evidence to suspect Chair Powell of a crime; indeed, its justifications are so thin and unsubstantiated that the Court can only conclude that they are pretextual," wrote Boasberg.

"This ruling confirms just how weak and frivolous the criminal investigation of Chairman Powell is and it is nothing more than a failed attack on Fed independence," Sen. Thom Tillis said in a social media post.

"Appealing the ruling will only delay the confirmation of Kevin Warsh as the next Fed Chair," said Tillis in the post. The U.S. Senator for North Carolina is said to have vowed to continue blocking President Donald Trump's nominee to replace Powell, Kevin Warsh, as chair until the investigation of Powell is resolved.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: ServiceNow CEO says AI could push jobless rate into 30% range for new grads
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) CEO Bill McDermott said the proliferation of AI in the workplace could soon push the unemployment rate for young college graduates into the mid-30% range, according to CNBC.

McDermott told CNBC on Friday that the jobless rate for new college grads “could easily go into the mid-30s in the next couple of years.”

“So much of the work is going to be done by agents. So it’s going to be challenging for young people to differentiate themselves in the corporate environment,” McDermott added.

The jobless rate for recent college graduates was around 5.7% at the end of last year, with the underemployment rate reaching its highest level since 2020 at 42.5%, according to CNBC, which cited the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

data.title: Air Products, Celanese, Nutrien all upgraded at Wells Fargo as beneficiaries of Iran war
data.description:
Air Products (APD), Celanese (CE) and Nutrien were all upgraded Friday to Overweight from Equal Weight at Wells Fargo, which expects higher pricing for commodity chemical and fertilizer chains will lead to upwardly revised estimated H1 2026 outlooks.

Regarding Air Products (APD), helium spot pricing has soared after a third of global capacity was removed after QatarEnergy halted liquefied natural gas production at the massive Ras Laffan complex; helium is extracted as a byproduct of natural gas processing, so any disruption to LNG output directly cuts helium supply.

As a result, Wells Fargo's Michael Sison believes other LNG producers will ramp up capacity in an effort to fill the supply shortfall, which should benefit industrial gas producers that supply LNG production.

In Air Products' (APD) previous earnings release, the company guided for helium to be a 4% headwind to estimated FY 2026 EPS, but Sison now says the impact could be far less, prompting him to upgrade the stock and raise his price target to $325 from $270, based on his $5.4B EBITDA estimate and 16x estimated 2026 EV/EBITDA, a multiple that is near the midpoint of APD's seven-year range of 12x-17x.

Sison said Celanese's (CE) Acetyl Chain segment should be supported by recent increases in acetic acid prices amid feedstock availability concerns due to the Iran war; additionally, as a U.S. Gulf Coast producer, the analyst thinks the company stands to benefit to a greater degree than producers in China and the Middle East, given they are not exposed to current feedstock supply issues or substantial feedstock cost inflation.

The analyst also upgraded Nutrien (NTR) in response to the recent run-up in urea prices, which have jumped more than 25% since the end of February, and said he believes strong positive pricing momentum will continue as long as the conflict persists and potentially for a short period thereafter; he raises his stock PT to $100 from $77 based on 10x estimated 2026 EV/EBITDA, which is equal to just 6.5x mid-cycle EBTIDA of $7B.

data.title: Microsoft becomes first cloud provider to begin validating Nvidia's Vera Rubin NVL72 system
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) Azure has become the first cloud service provider to begin validating Nvidia's (NVDA) Vera Rubin NVL72 system, according to CEO Satya Nadella.

"We're the first cloud to bring up an NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 system for validation, another big step in building the next generation of AI infrastructure with NVIDIA," Nadella said in a post on X Friday afternoon.

Microsoft's long-time partnership with Nvidia has allowed the company to better implement successive generations of their hardware in its data centers.

"Microsoft has years of market-proven experience in designing and deploying scalable AI infrastructure that evolves with every major advancement of AI technology," said Rani Borkar, Microsoft's president of Azure Hardware Systems. "In lockstep with each successive generation of NVIDIA's accelerated compute infrastructure, Microsoft rapidly integrates NVIDIA's innovations and delivers them at scale."

"The NVIDIA Rubin platform marks a major step forward in accelerated computing, and Azure's AI data centers and superfactories are already engineered to take full advantage," Borkar added. "Years of co-design with NVIDIA across interconnects, memory systems, thermals, packaging, and rack-scale architecture means Rubin integrates directly into Azure's platform without rework."

Although Microsoft said it is the first to bring the Vera Rubin up for validation, multiple cloud providers are expected to deploy the technology in 2026. This includes Amazon Web Services (AMZN), CoreWeave (CRWV), Google (GOOG)(GOOGL), Nebius (NBIS), and Oracle (ORCL).

Each Vera Rubin NVL72 rack provides up to 3.6 exaflops of performance, which is about five times more than the GB200-based systems, according to Nvidia. Each rack combines 72 GPUs and 36 CPUs connected through sixth-generation NVLink, which provides 260 terabytes per second of bandwidth.

data.title: Savara GAAP EPS of -$0.13
data.description:
• Savara press release (SVRA): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.13.

  • Research and development expenses for the fourth quarter of 2025 and 2024 were $20.9 million and $23.3 million, respectively.

    General and administrative expenses for the fourth quarter of 2025 and 2024 were $12.5 million and $7.8 million, respectively.

    As of December 31, 2025, the Company had cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments of $235.7 million.

    Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results

    The Company’s net loss for the year ended December 31, 2025 was $118.8 million, or $(0.53) per share, compared with a net loss of was $95.9 million, or $(0.48) per share for the year ended December 31, 2024.

    Research and development expenses increased $3.4 million, or 4.3%, to $81.4 million for the year ended December 31, 2025 from $78.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. This increase was primarily due to the performance of tasks related to our MOLBREEVI program, which includes $5.7 million of costs related to regulatory affairs and quality assurance, primarily driven by the BLA submission; $0.5 million of costs related to our chemistry, manufacturing, and controls activities; and $1.0 million other departmental overhead, partially offset by a decrease of $3.8 million in clinical costs.

    General and administrative expenses increased $17.0 million, or 68.0%, to $42.1 million for the year ended December 31, 2025 from $25.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.

data.title: Activate Energy Acquisition Corp. reports FY results
data.description:
• Activate Energy Acquisition Corp. press release (AEAQ.UN): FY Net income of $ 300,371 for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, primarily driven by interest income.
  • Cash and Investments: Approximately $ 230,556,356 held in the trust account, invested in U.S. government securities or money market funds meeting certain conditions under Rule 2a-7 of the Investment Company Act.

data.title: AMD ownership increases, Synopsys sees a decrease as fund managers alter portfolios
data.description:
The semiconductor industry has received notable attention from investors, given how much of the artificial intelligence spending boom has benefited the industry. And Bank of America pointed to recent data from active fund managers making changes in their portfolios as worthy of attention.

According to the investment firm's February Equity Strategy data, the relative weighting for the industry continued to decline among active managers, down to 0.84x, the lowest since June 2012, and down from 0.89x in January. 

Despite the declines in relative weighting, the compute area of semiconductors has seen the greatest increase in ownership, led by AMD (AMD). Fund managers increased their position in AMD by 1,001 basis points on a quarter-over-quarter basis, Bank of America said.

Conversely, analog chip makers saw an average decline of 51 basis points quarter-over-quarter and 254 basis points year-over-year, with Onsemi (ON) and Microchip (MCHP) seeing 81 basis point declines. Analog Devices (ADI) was the only analog stock to see a sequential rise, up 6 basis points. 

AMD's rise, Synopsys' fall

Delving deeper, Bank of America said that AMD is now owned by 35% of funds and is the third-most widely owned chip stock behind Nvidia (NVDA) (at 78%) and Broadcom (AVGO) (at 74%). However, it is still underweight compared to the S&P 500, at 15th among semiconductors.

On the flip side, Synopsys (SNPS) has seen the greatest decrease in ownership, down 406 basis points quarter-over-quarter, at 26% of fund managers. However, it is still the second most overweight semiconductor stock, at 1.71x relative weight, behind KLA Corp. (KLAC) at 1.79x. Lam Research (LRCX) has seen an increase in ownership, up 670 basis points quarter-over-quarter to 33% of fund managers, now the fourth highest in the group.

Other takes

Other notable takeaways from the survey include: Teradyne (TER) seeing a 28% decrease quarter-over-quarter in its relative weight, to 0.30x from 0.47x in January 2026; ownership in Micron (MU) increasing to 25% from 18%, amid the continued rise in memory prices. However, Micron's relative weight was down 5% quarter-over-quarter to 1.24x, the lowest in over a year but still the fifth highest in semiconductors.

Other findings include Lumentum (LITE) and Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) having the most short interest (16% and 14% of the float), while Nvidia and Broadcom have the least, at roughly 1% apiece. 

Long-only investors had higher net weighting in Skyworks, Qualcomm (QCOM), NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), Broadcom, and Texas Instruments (TXN), compared to hedge funds, which were more overweight in Teradyne, Lam Research, and Onsemi. Both long-only investors and hedge funds were overweight Applied Materials, Cadence Design Systems (CDNS), and Synopsys, the survey showed.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: MRVL, PM, ANET, DKNG
data.description:
Bay Area Ideas upgraded Arista Networks (ANET) following valuation contraction and improved long-term prospects, while Gary Alexander moved DraftKings (DKNG) to Neutral after a significant share price decline and the launch of its new “super app.” On the downgrade side, Cash Flow Venue moved Philip Morris (PM) to Hold citing regulatory headwinds and premium valuation, and Deep Value Investing downgraded Marvell Technology (MRVL) as custom silicon growth guidance disappointed relative to AI industry leaders.

Upgrades

  • Arista Networks (ANET): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Bay Area Ideas. The analyst cites a contraction in valuation and improved long-term prospects, with robust demand evidenced by purchase commitments increasing 42% quarter-over-quarter despite near-term supply constraints.

“I would say that despite the supply constraints and softer than desired 2026 guidance, Arista Networks' positioning is quite strong for the long term.”

  • DraftKings (DKNG): Upgrade to Neutral by Gary Alexander. Following a 30%+ decline in share price, the stock’s valuation has become more attractive, with the launch of a consolidated “super app” expected to drive iGaming growth and cross-promotion efficiency in 2026.

“Amid strong recent Q4 results, I do think DraftKings is now a more balanced bag of positives and negatives, with risks outweighing opportunities. I'm upping my rating on this stock to "Neutral.”

Downgrades

  • Philip Morris International (PM): Downgrade to Hold by Cash Flow Venue. Despite leading the smoke-free movement with smoke-free products comprising 41.5% of results, the analyst cites valuation concerns and India’s ban on heated tobacco products as limiting near-term upside.

“I'm not surprised with a downward pressure on PM's stock price given recent developments on that front. Also, I feel that investors are concerned with the large valuation premium PM holds over MO, which, in my view, limits the upside potential.”

  • Marvell Technology (MRVL): Downgrade to Hold by Deep Value Investing. The analyst is concerned that custom silicon/XPU growth guidance of 20% year-over-year for FY27 looks too modest compared to stronger AI leaders like Broadcom, despite raised total revenue guidance.

“I am downgrading to a hold as I see a painful uphill battle against stronger competitors in both the XPU and optics space. I think money will be chasing growth in the semiconductor industry (once the rotation out of tech comes to an end), and the likes of Broadcom look far more compelling than Marvell from an AI growth perspective.”

data.title: SA Asks: Which energy stocks are most exposed to the Strait of Hormuz crisis?
data.description:
Which energy stocks are most exposed to a closure of the Strait of Hormuz amid the U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict?

Seeking Alpha analysts Fluidsdoc and Grassroots Trading weigh in.

Fluidsdoc: Closure of the strait is causing disruption to the movement of oil and LNG out of the Persian Gulf to markets in the west. Qatar's operations, in particular, have been impacted by this threat. Early in the conflict, it chose to shut down its LNG facilities at Ras Laffan, through which it processes and exports 77 mpta (20% of global capacity) of the product. 

Companies whose earnings statements could be impacted by this event include ConocoPhillips (COP), ExxonMobil (XOM), Shell (SHEL), and TotalEnergies (TTE). These companies own interests in various phases of projects that feed into Ras Laffan, including Qatargas 3, North Field South, and North Field Expansion. 

That said, these companies are all so huge that the investment thesis for them would not be seriously affected in the short term.

Grassroots Trading: The companies that would be most directly affected by the Strait of Hormuz closure are the ones that produce oil and export LNG from the Persian Gulf.

This is especially true for companies connected to Qatar’s LNG exports and oil/gas production in the Gulf region, because their shipments have to pass through the Strait of Hormuz to get to global markets. Examples include Shell (SHEL), TotalEnergies (TTE), and BP (BP).  Meanwhile, even if Exxon Mobil (XOM) doesn't ship all the natural gas itself, its partnership in related projects means it could still be indirectly affected as gas shipments through that region continue to face disruptions.

That said, large integrated oil companies operate in many segments of the energy business, so they're generally diversified enough to benefit as oil prices rise. In contrast, companies that focus mainly on upstream activities, such as exploration and production, and don't have refining or chemical operations to balance their businesses would likely see the sharpest earnings volatility. ConocoPhillips (COP), Occidental Petroleum (OXY), EOG Resources (EOG), Devon Energy (DVN), and APA (APA) come to mind.
